location.description: PostgreSQL connection URI: postgres://[user[:password]@]host[:port][/database]. If a database path is included, it is used as the target for pg_restore when restoring single-database dumps.

host.description: PostgreSQL server hostname (default: localhost). Overrides the host in the location URI.

port.description: PostgreSQL server port (default: 5432). Overrides the port in the location URI.

username.description: PostgreSQL username. Overrides the username in the location URI.

password.description: PostgreSQL password. Overrides the password in the location URI.

database.description: Target database for pg_restore. If omitted, the database name is inferred from the dump filename. Not used with recreate (the name is taken from the archive metadata in that case).

databases.description: Comma-separated list of database names to restore. When set, only .dump files matching one of these names are restored; all others are skipped. Globals (00000-globals.sql) are always restored regardless of this filter (unless no_globals=true). Example: myapp,myother.

clean.description: When enabled, passes --clean --if-exists to pg_restore, dropping objects within the target database before recreating them. The database must already exist. Mutually exclusive with recreate.

recreate.description: When enabled, passes -C --clean --if-exists to pg_restore: the entire target database is dropped and recreated from the archive metadata. The target database name is taken from the archive. The 'postgres' database is never dropped — it is restored with --clean --if-exists instead, mirroring pg_dumpall behaviour. Mutually exclusive with clean.

no_globals.description: When enabled, skips feeding 00000-globals.sql to psql. By default globals are restored automatically when present.

no_owner.description: When enabled, passes --no-owner to pg_restore so that ALTER OWNER commands are skipped. Useful when the roles from the source server do not exist on the target server.

exit_on_error.description: When enabled, passes -e to pg_restore and ON_ERROR_STOP=1 to psql so restore stops on the first error.

schema_only.description: When enabled, passes -s to pg_restore to restore only schema (no data). Mutually exclusive with data_only.

data_only.description: When enabled, passes -a to pg_restore to restore only data (no schema). Mutually exclusive with schema_only.

pg_bin_dir.description: Directory containing the PostgreSQL client binaries (pg_restore, psql). When omitted, binaries are resolved via $PATH.

ssl_mode.description: SSL mode for the connection, passed via PGSSLMODE.

ssl_cert.description: Path to the client SSL certificate file (PEM format), passed via PGSSLCERT. Mutually exclusive with ssl_cert_data.

ssl_cert_data.description: Inline PEM content of the client SSL certificate. Written to a temporary file at runtime. Mutually exclusive with ssl_cert.

ssl_key.description: Path to the client SSL private key file (PEM format), passed via PGSSLKEY. Mutually exclusive with ssl_key_data.

ssl_key_data.description: Inline PEM content of the client SSL private key. Written to a temporary file at runtime. Mutually exclusive with ssl_key.

ssl_root_cert.description: Path to the root CA certificate used to verify the server (PEM format), passed via PGSSLROOTCERT. Mutually exclusive with ssl_root_cert_data.

ssl_root_cert_data.description: Inline PEM content of the root CA certificate. Written to a temporary file at runtime. Mutually exclusive with ssl_root_cert.
